name: Sir John Soane's Museum
meta_description: Opening Times and Ticketed Tours We are normally open Wednesday to Sunday, 10am to 5pm (last admission is usually 4:30pm, however if the Museum is at capacity we may have to close admissions earlier). We are closed Monday and Tuesday, but remain open for bank holidays. Entry is free. There is no need to pre-book – you can walk in on the day. The Museum has limited space and is very popular, so you may need to wait for admission which can lead to delayed entry. If you wish to visit as a group of eight or more, you will need to get in touch with us. The opening times for the panels in the Picture Room are as follows: 2pm: A Rake’s Progress (north wall)11am, 3pm and 4pm: Picture Room Recess (south wall) Sir John Soane’s Museum is one of the world’s great architectural masterpieces housing an astonishing collection of art, sculpture, artefacts, and architectural fragments, all of which are displayed in Soane’s remarkable historic interiors. We do not currently operate a pre-booked system, but if you would like to guarantee entry at a certain time you are welcome to book one of our ticketed Highlights Tours Tours which take place daily at midday, with an additional tour at 10am on Fridays, and 11am on Saturdays and Sundays.
title: Plan Your Visit | Sir John Soane's Museum
